description_long: This bungalow is a long, rectangular residential building with a prominent gable roof covered in gray shingles. The facade features a series of symmetrical, rectangular windows with blue-tinted glass, framed by beige trim, and several protruding entryways or porches. The exterior walls are primarily blue with beige horizontal bands, giving the structure a modern yet classic appearance. The roofline is punctuated by small gable accents above the main entrances, adding architectural interest. The building appears to be a single story, with no visible upper floors or balconies. The overall design suggests a spacious, multi-unit dwelling suitable for suburban or urban neighborhoods.
description: A long, single-story bungalow with a gray gable roof, blue and beige exterior, and symmetrical window arrangements. Features multiple entryways and a modern, classic design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrances, symmetrical windows, and gable roof accents.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the building and the continuous gable roof.
description_view_2: Rear view with evenly spaced windows and a simple, uninterrupted facade.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring the length and roof structure seen in view 1.
